sigma_analysis_results.rule_description:
Detects execution of the builtin "del"/"erase" commands in order to delete files.
Adversaries may delete files left behind by the actions of their intrusion activity.
Malware, tools, or other non-native files dropped or created on a system by an adversary may leave traces to indicate to what was done within a network and how.
Removal of these files can occur during an intrusion, or as part of a post-intrusion process to minimize the adversary's footprint.

attributes.magic: PE32 executable (GUI) Intel 80386, for MS Windows

products: Dell Systems Management Tools and Documentation

crowdsourced_ai_results.analysis: The analyzed binary is a standard implementation of the Windows host process 'rundll32.exe'. Its primary purpose is to load dynamic-link libraries (DLLs) and execute their exported functions as specified via command-line arguments. Key behaviors include parsing the command line (?RunDLL_ParseCommand), loading modules (?_LoadCommandModule) using LoadLibraryExW, resolving entry points (?_FindCommandFunction) via GetProcAddress, and executing them (CallRunDllFunction). The binary includes legitimate Microsoft manifests, handles WOW64 redirection, and manages UAC virtualization (SHIsCurrentAppElevated). No malicious functionality or suspicious obfuscation was identified within the binary itself; it is a standard system utility.
